reviews.review_description: You’ll definitely get a decent workout from this game. The cyborgs are intimidating and unpredictable enough to keep you on edge. My heart rate always gets really high.

reviews.review_title: fun , play all time for years
reviews.review_description: fun workout boxing game. play every other day, good workout game.

I mainly got this because of the ability to upload your own mp3s and I like the idea of fighting robots rhythmically. Currently though, it's a bit of a mess. 

While the game immediately found my mp3 files, it doesn't seem to read them as mp3s, as in it just shows the file name, not the artist or anything like that.  Since many of the files start with a track number, it's a nightmare trying to find a song.

Also, even after starting a song, I fight two bots and the game is over. The track is still going.

Here's what could make this game great:
- Better file reading / organization for mp3s
- A randomization option for choosing music
- A session timer (i.e. set how long I want to fight and see how many robots you can knock out in that time)

I was really impressed by the graphics and the mixed reality. This is the first fitness game that I liked in mixed reality, actually. This game really could be one of the best with a few additions.

Some other reviews mention that you are forced to do the mixed reality. I don't like mixed reality either but you don't have to opt in for mixed reality. I like the ring side settings the best. It has a lot of options.

For a game that purports to be a boxing simulator, it does a really poor job. Every opponent fights with the same simple strategy, which is to punch every few seconds. They don't defend themselves as well, they don't move around, and blocking their punches seems to work or not work randomly. Yes you can work up a sweat but there's not much else to enjoy here. The novelty of having a giant robot in your house wears off quickly, and there are only a few robots which are somewhat blocky and low-res texture mapped. It's not satisfying to him them. They don't get slower or wobbly as they take more hits. And when they are defeated, they simply vaporize and get replaced with another opponent. There is one non-robot, but it's a guy wearing VR so you can't look him in the eye, and the green skin is creepy. If you want a good boxing simulator, get Thrill of the Fight. If you want a good arcade boxing simulator, get Creed. At least then you will be entertained while getting a workout.

Really hate this one. I feel like I am being force fed mixes reality by this game, and Meta in general. I can't find a setting to turn down the music. Basically I would like the opposite of this, a visual environment (why I got a VR headset) without the music so I can play my own music in the room. 

The fighting is not well executed, matches are short, and I feel like its just blocking a few punches until the magic knockout target comes up.  I can't think of any reason I would open this X-Fighter app over any of the other fighters I've been using for years. 

This one is not for me.

Wow what an amazing workout, certainly gets the blood pumping 😂 the graphics are amazing! It genuinely looks like you have a giant mecha in front of you.

There's a nice wide variety of music tracks and I think it's great how each robot has it's own unique fighting style. Green can be a bit eager! I think he gets up far too close. I couldn't see what I was aiming for and hit myself in the face! 😂🤣

I like how you can add your own music that's an excellent touch. And pass through mode is a wonderful idea! I feel like I can let loose more without fear of damaging anything. Great if you have limited space too🥊😁 

The tutorial was easy to follow and the U.I is laid out in a way that's smooth to navigate. Not once did I feel lost in menus and found it quick and simple to get into the action. 

In a future update I would love to see a wider selection of robots to fight against and more game modes!  Overall I'd definitely give this game five stars. It's an excellent rhythm fitness game with a very original spin that keeps me coming back for more. 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

reviews.review_description: This game is clever, it's like a rythym boxing game. I love punching to the beat! There are teams of cyborgs you take on, and they all fight a bit differently. Lots of great music already included but you can add your own as well! This will definitely count as your cardio for the day after a few rounds. I was sweating while I was swinging! Will be adding this to my Quest workout list! I highly recommend checking this one out!

I love the mixed reality option. No more worries about breaking something when I'm playing.

100% recommend everyone purchasing this game!!

reviews.review_description:
My thoughts on the game: I'm in aww by the graphics and the way it feels when you punch a character but the whole rhythm game mechanics takes me out of it. Honestly just get rid of the whole rhythm thing and make this into an actual fighting game like thrill of the fight, or at least make that type of gameplay available as a separate option. 

(Simplicity is best, that's why thrill of the fight was so successful and it built upon real boxing and that's also why thrill of the fight is also better than creed. This has the potential to be better than both of them.) 

This game could be really great with the AR mode and AAA graphics and animations as well as the hit physics it would an instant HIT 100%. (The AR mode is something special that's for sure.) If you wanted something more arcade like. I would imagine you could make something similar to a Street Fighter 2 type of game except in VR and it would be spectacular. 

So much potential for this. But currently I only give it 3 stars. Great graphics. Nice fighting physics. Not a fan of the game mechanics. Hoping for great things for the future on this one however.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. I wanted to clarify that hitting in rhythm is not mandatory, it simply provides additional bonuses. We understand the rhythm aspect might not appeal to everyone, so we'll endeavor to incorporate a gameplay style more independent of music in the future. We truly value your suggestions and look forward to enhancing your experience with our game.

reviews.review_description: Got x-booster long time ago and love it , with this game x-fighter is my fitness day complete, GOOD JOB !

reviews.review_description:
X-fitness has been part of my go to work out routine for the last year or so and when I saw they had a new game/workout available I had to try it — and am I glad I did! 
This is a fun workout with lots of music options built in and the ability to add your own MP3’s and the mixed reality option so you can see your surroundings while you fight is a great addition.
I definitely recommend this as one of your VR workout apps!
(One suggestion is it would be nice to get a punch count at the end to see how many you landed/ blocked or were hit with.)

reviews.review_description: Fighting big ugly robots in my living room is the best mixed reality so far.  Yes, this is a rhythm game.  Some reviewers complain about this.  To me, this is the best part.  And did I say it's a sweat-worthy workout?
Great fitness game, fighting robots in my room it's a really nice experience. Dynamic music and smooth but exhausting gameplay. I have a great time with this one.

reviews.review_description:
For the very first time I have a physical fighting game and don't need to be afraid to accidentally  smash the Controller into Walls or closets. The oppenents are super nicely integrated into my room and after a few Fights I am completely breathless.

Don't know what it looks like in a few weeks but by now I am absolutely excited and it's totally what I was looking for for a long time

Boxing robots to your own music in pass through. Doesn't get much better. X-booster/x-fitness is awesome due to the fact you could use your own music easily. This takes it to a new level.

No more hitting fairly repetitive targets. You are using your boxing skills against robots, all with different fighting styles. Fight however you want. 

I can't wait to see this evolve. I would love to see more robots added. It would also benefit from having the ability to create multiple playlists. 

Well worth the purchase for any boxing or VR fitness fan.

reviews.review_description:
A few reviews are missing the point of it being a cardio rhythm hitter rather than a boxing sim like Creed or Thrill. 
I also have had their other title x-fitness a good while which had some good improvements made.
This needs the 360⁰ bot circling as an option urgently. It's fundamental to be able to switch it off - I cannot reengineer my room to suit the game (x-fitness doesn't do this either so no reason x-fighter should).
It also seems to encroach on your space leading to unblockable strikes - care needs to account for smaller room spaces, not just empty coding offices and huge houses. Distance options would be extremely useful here rather than needing our repeated Meta button repositioning every time.
It also could use more imagination and personality in robot behaviours and changes to our attacks (eg see X-fitness's range of weapons and SFX).
Has great but unutilised promise and recommended with these caveats. It's a unique MR title.

It’s the thrill of the fight meets giant robots (and your living room).

Very cool to be able to fight giant robots in pass through. I feel like it’s safer than TTOTF as well because you can see your surroundings.

I definitely recommend it.
